data.content: The project uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MSTest 3.8.0 includes TRX support). Let me run the tests with `--report-trx`:
data.reasoningText:
This is using Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MSTest 3.8.0 with `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`). To produce a TRX report with `dotnet test`, I need to pass the appropriate logger. With the new testing platform, TRX reporting requires the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` package.

Let me check if that package is needed. With MSTest 3.8.0 and Microsoft.Testing.Platform, TRX reporting is available via `--report-trx` command line option, but the package `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` needs to be referenced.


Actually, starting with MSTest 3.4+, the TRX report extension is included in the MSTest meta-package, so with version 3.8.0 it should come transitively. I can just run `dotnet test --report-trx` to generate the TRX output.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4ba0a996a82e46c98279e253922a527f && dotnet test --report-trx 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with TRX report", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content: The `--report-trx` flag needs to be passed through to the test platform via `--`:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with TRX report via passthrough

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with TRX report via passthrough

data.reasoningText:
The `--report-trx` flag is not recognized by `dotnet test` in this SDK version. This is because `--report-trx` is a Microsoft.Testing.Platform flag that gets passed to the test executable, not to MSBuild. With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, the way to pass these flags is via `--` separator.

Let me try: `dotnet test -- --report-trx`

arguments.description: Run tests with TRX report via passthrough
